message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>Coronavirus disease has become a worldwide threat affecting almost every country in the world. The aim of this study is to identify the COVID-19 cases (positive, recovery and death) in Algeria using the Double Exponential Smoothing Method and an Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) model for forecasting the COVID-19 cases.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  The data for this study were obtained from March 21
                  <jats:sup>st</jats:sup>
                  , 2020 to November 26
                  <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                  , 2020. The daily Algerian COVID-19 confirmed cases were sourced from The Ministry of Health, Population and Hospital Reform of Algeria. Based on the results of PACF, ACF, and estimated parameters of the ARIMA model in the COVID-19 case in Algeria following the ARIMA model (0,1,1). Observed cases during the forecast period were accurately predicted and were placed within the prediction intervals generated by the fitted model. This study shows that ARIMA models with optimally selected covariates are useful tools for monitoring and predicting trends of COVID-19 cases in Algeria.
                </jats:p>
title: Forecasting daily confirmed COVID-19 cases in Algeria using ARIMA models
